title: Neural Network as a Specialty Processor (Software 2.0)
description: Treating a neural network as a specialty processor or a new kind of computer is not controversial; it is a well-known, widely accepted framework in modern AI engineering. Famously articulated by Andrej Karpathy as 'Software 2.0', the concept draws direct parallels between traditional hardware and AI models. In 'Software 1.0', a CPU executes explicit instructions written by a programmer. In 'Software 2.0', the Neural Network acts as the processor. The 'code' is the abstract weights compiled by an optimization algorithm against a dataset. The Prompt acts as the instruction set or input, the Context Window serves as the RAM, and the model itself is the processor executing probabilistic logic.
